algorithmDebateRecord.dcterms:title: Code-Dependent: Pros and Cons of the Algorithm Age
algorithmDebateRecord.dcterms:creator: Lee Rainie
Janna Anderson
algorithmDebateRecord.dcterms:description: A Pew Research Center report about experts' views on the benefits and risks of algorithms in society.
algorithmDebateRecord.claim: Algorithms can save lives, make things easier, and help organize complex information.
Algorithms can also increase corporate and governmental control, produce biased results, create filter bubbles, reduce choice and serendipity, and worsen digital divides.
algorithmDebateRecord.supportingEvidence: The source states that algorithms can save lives, make things easier, and conquer chaos.
The source states that experts worry algorithms can put too much control in the hands of corporations and governments, perpetuate bias, create filter bubbles, and worsen digital divides.
